title: The Ultimate Software Evaluation Checklist
description: Never miss a critical feature with our comprehensive evaluation framework.

sections.title: Define Business Requirements
sections.body: Document the core problems you are trying to solve and the workflows the software must support. Involve stakeholders from every affected department to capture diverse needs. Clear requirements prevent you from being swayed by flashy features you will never use.

sections.title: Set a Budget
sections.body: Determine both your initial investment and the total cost of ownership over two to three years. Include licensing, implementation, training, and ongoing support costs. A realistic budget helps you filter out options that are too expensive or lackluster for your spend level.

sections.title: Create a Shortlist
sections.body: Research the market and narrow candidates to three to five tools that meet your core requirements. Read reviews on trusted platforms and ask peers in your industry for recommendations. A focused shortlist saves time and prevents analysis paralysis.

sections.title: Request Demos
sections.body: Ask vendors to walk through your specific use cases rather than a generic product tour. Prepare a checklist of must-see features and scenarios beforehand. A targeted demo reveals whether the software truly fits your needs or just looks polished on the surface.

sections.title: Evaluate Support
sections.body: Check each vendor's support channels, hours of operation, and response time SLAs. Read user reviews about support quality and browse their knowledge base to assess self-service resources. Reliable support is critical — even the best software runs into issues eventually.

sections.title: Check References
sections.body: Ask vendors for customer references in your industry or company size range. Prepare questions about implementation experience, uptime, and how the vendor handles feature requests. Speaking with real users gives you an honest picture you will never get from sales material.

author: StackPilot Team
